summary: First iteration: tested Scheiner Sports Solutions FastAPI backend + Streamlit frontend with seeded sessions. 13/13 backend pytest cases pass (health, /api/auth/me valid/invalid/no-auth, /api/auth/session invalid id, /api/auth/logout deletes session, /api/subscription/plan public 9.99, /api/subscription/checkout auth-gated + creates real Stripe URL + DB row, /api/subscription/status unknown id & no-auth, /api/webhook/stripe returns 4xx on bad signature without crashing). Frontend Streamlit flows tested with Playwright: unauthenticated login hero with Google button, paywall hero with $9.99 Subscribe with Stripe button, dashboard with all 8 tools, NRFI tool navigation works. One minor data-asset issue: NRFI tool surfaces 'No such file: data/nrfi.csv'.

minor.issue: When session_id is unknown to Stripe, endpoint may surface a 500 (Stripe call) rather than mapping cleanly to 404 first. Consider checking payment_transactions before hitting Stripe.

integration_issues.issue: Tool loads but shows runtime error 'No such file or directory: data/nrfi.csv' — required CSV data file is missing from repo/seed

action_items: Add data/nrfi.csv (and any other missing CSVs for the other 7 tools) so tools render real data instead of 'file not found' fallback.
Optional: in /api/subscription/status check payment_transactions first and 404 fast for unknown session_ids before calling Stripe.
Optional: webhook returns HTTPException(400) which is correct; consider also catching specific signature errors to log them distinctly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 121: subscription_status field is set the same in both branches of `status if is_active else status` — looks like a no-op ternary; harmless but should be cleaned up.

auth_gate.py: BACKEND_URL falls back to '/api' if env is missing which is fine, but `get_origin_url()` returns BACKEND_URL — Stripe success/cancel URLs would resolve to the public preview URL (good). No hardcoded URLs detected.

server.py uses CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True — Starlette will silently disallow credentialed CORS with wildcard origin. Not a functional blocker for current Streamlit-on-same-host setup but should be tightened.
All Mongo reads correctly exclude _id via projection — good.
Test sessions are seeded directly in DB and OAuth bypass via ?session_token= is documented in /app/auth_testing.md — works as designed for E2E testing.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on auth/paywall/dashboard flow; NRFI tool navigates but data CSV missing

test_credentials: Seeded sessions present in db sss_db: test_session_noSub_abc (no sub), test_session_withSub_xyz (active sub). Documented in /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: No new seeds created. Pre-existing seeded users/sessions/subscriptions in sss_db verified intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Emergent OAuth + Stripe checkout + subscription gating endpoints verified working with seeded sessions. Frontend Streamlit renders correct hero per auth/subscription state. The login button + subscribe button + tool buttons are reachable. The only outstanding functional gap is that the 8 tool pages need their underlying data CSVs (NRFI confirmed missing data/nrfi.csv). To re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. To re-verify frontend, open the preview URL with ?session_token=test_session_withSub_xyz or test_session_noSub_abc.
rca of the issue: NRFI tool failure RCA: streamlit page (likely /app/pages/*.py) does pd.read_csv('data/nrfi.csv') with a relative path; that data file does not exist in repo. Mitigation: ship a seed CSV or fetch from upstream source on startup. Not an auth/subscription bug.
